title: Tech giants and regulators clash

slides.headline: Tech giants and regulators clash
slides.subtitle: From AI chips to robotaxi setbacks, here is your tech news roundup.

slides.headline: FCC chief targets broadcasters
slides.explanation: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr is threatening broadcasters who make jokes about the president. This raises serious questions about how government power impacts free speech on our airwaves.

slides.headline: AMD challenges Nvidia in AI
slides.explanation: AMD is launching a new rack-scale system to compete with Nvidia in the AI chip market. This move could change the hardware landscape for companies building massive AI tools.

slides.headline: ChatGPT gets a health upgrade
slides.explanation: OpenAI has opened its health-focused ChatGPT features to all users in the US. You can now pull in data from apps like Apple Health to get more personalized insights.

slides.headline: Tesla robotaxi miles drop
slides.explanation: Tesla says its paid robotaxi miles fell 36 percent last quarter despite adding new cities. It is a surprising dip for a company betting heavily on autonomous driving.

slides.headline: Google hit with massive EU fine
slides.explanation: The EU fined Google 890 million euros for favoring its own apps over competitors. This is the first major penalty under new rules designed to keep the tech market fair.

caption:
From major regulatory fines to shifts in the AI chip market, today's tech landscape is moving fast. We have broken down the five biggest stories you need to know to stay informed. Which of these updates caught your attention the most?

Follow @headlinne for a daily brief. More at HEADLINNE.com.

title: Oil Prices and AI Security Risks

slides.headline: Oil Prices and AI Security Risks
slides.subtitle: Five quick updates on the markets and tech trends you need to know today.

slides.headline: AI hacks are a wake-up call
slides.explanation: A co-founder at Hugging Face says companies are still unprepared for rogue AI models. This matters because the security landscape for businesses has fundamentally shifted.

slides.headline: Markets react to $100 oil
slides.explanation: Stocks dropped after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel despite previous geopolitical tensions. It shows that investors can no longer ignore the economic impact of rising energy costs.

slides.headline: Mapping China's AI sector
slides.explanation: There is a massive landscape of Chinese AI companies ranging from chip makers to cloud providers. Understanding these players is key to tracking the global tech race.

slides.headline: Island gets help with power bills
slides.explanation: The States of Guernsey said it would give Alderney Electricity £200,000 to offset islanders' bills.

slides.headline: New global deal on open-source AI
slides.explanation: Nations at an APEC summit have agreed to back open-source AI with stronger security measures. It is the first time ministers have formally included this topic in a major joint statement.

caption:
From rising energy costs to the evolving security risks of AI, here is a snapshot of the stories moving the needle today. Staying informed doesn't have to take all day. Which of these updates surprised you the most?

Follow @headlinne for a daily brief. More at HEADLINNE.com.
